headline: Nvidia’s Trillion Dollar AI Goal And Groq Deal Reframe Valuation
summary: Nvidia (NasdaqGS:NVDA) has raised its 2027 AI revenue projection to $1 trillion, up from $500 billion previously. The company announced a $20b acquisition of Groq assets, including Groq 3 Language Processing Units. Groq 3 LPUs are set to be integrated into Nvidia’s upcoming Rubin rack scale systems alongside Blackwell chips. Nvidia now pairs this larger AI ambition with a share price of $177.39 and a 5 year return of 1,135.0%. Over the past year, the stock is up 88.1%, with a 5.9% gain over...

headline: Foxconn first-quarter revenue jumps 30% y/y
summary: Taiwan's Foxconn, the ‌world's largest contract ‌electronics maker and Nvidia's ​biggest server maker, reported on Sunday a jump of ‌29.7% in ⁠first-quarter revenue year on year, ⁠fuelled by strong AI-related demand. Revenue for ​the quarter ​from ​January to ‌March was T$2.13 trillion ($66.60 billion), Foxconn said in a statement, versus an LSEG ‌SmartEstimate of ​T$2.148 trillion ​that ​gives greater ‌weight to forecasts from ​analysts ​who are more consistently accurate. ($1 = 31.9800 ​Taiwan ‌dollars)
